headline: ALASKA GOLD JUNIOR LAUNCHES $63M, 60,000M DRILLING BLITZ — LUCKY SHOT FEASIBILITY DUE 2027
snippet: Contango Silver & Gold (NYSE: CTGO) unveiled a fully funded 2026 exploration program covering Lucky Shot, Johnson Tract, and Kitsault Valley in Alaska and northern BC, backed by $9M in JV cash from the Peak Gold joint venture. The 60,000m campaign targets a Lucky Shot feasibility study by H1 2027; the project earlier drilled 60.22 g/t gold over 5.92m this cycle.

headline: BMO HIKES GOLD EQUITY TARGETS 15%, COPPER TARGETS 19% — CALLS MARKET A 'STRUCTURAL BULL RUN'
snippet: BMO Capital Markets lifted its gold equity price targets an average of 15% and copper equity targets 19%, citing record free cash flow margins and compressed multiples in what the bank calls a structural bull market. Top picks: Aris Mining (TSX: ARIS), Discovery Silver (TSX: DSV), Montage Gold (TSX: MAU), and Newmont (NYSE: NEM).

headline: GREENLAND APPROVES TRANSFER: CRITICAL METALS LOCKS IN 92.5% OF TANBREEZ — WORLD-CLASS HEAVY RARE EARTH DEPOSIT
snippet: Greenland approved the final 50.5% stake transfer in Tanbreez Mining to Critical Metals Corp., giving the company majority control of a deposit valued at ~$3B across 4.7 billion tonnes containing all eight critical heavy rare earth elements. Stock surged 23.6% pre-market; pilot plant operations are set for May 2026 with a 150-tonne bulk sample program to follow in June.

headline: NEXGEN URANIUM DISCOVERY GROWS: PATTERSON CORRIDOR EAST HIGH-GRADE ZONE EXPANDS 33% TO 550M VERTICAL
snippet: NexGen Energy (TSX: NXE) completed its 2026 winter drill program at Patterson Corridor East with the high-grade subdomain now at 550m vertical extent — a 33% expansion — and 210m strike length. Key hole RK-26-280c1 hit off-scale radioactivity (>61,000 cps) at 834m depth; the 42,000m summer program begins the week of May 25.

headline: GR SILVER HITS 1,395 G/T SILVER OVER 2.5M AT SAN MARCIAL — HIGH-GRADE ZONE CONFIRMED 500M DOWN-DIP
snippet: GR Silver Mining (TSXV: GRSL) reported first results from its 20,000m step-out program at San Marcial, Mexico: drill hole SMS26-03 returned 15.6m at 351 g/t Ag including 2.5m at 1,395 g/t Ag, confirming high-grade silver continuity 500m down-dip from surface. The intercept also opens new exploration potential above the main hydrothermal breccias in a previously interpreted barren zone.

headline: VIZSLA SILVER AWARDS $170M EPCM CONTRACT FOR PANUCO — MINE DESIGN AND ENGINEERING UNDERWAY IN MEXICO
snippet: Vizsla Silver (TSX: VZLA) awarded the EPCM contract for its Panuco silver-gold project in Sinaloa, Mexico to M3 Engineering & Technology for the $170M process plant and surface infrastructure, and a Mine Design contract to Mining Plus covering $50M in development capital. A formal production decision has not yet been made; detailed engineering and financing remain in progress.

headline: SILVER AT CRITICAL $75.20 SUPPORT; BANK OF AMERICA EYES $309/OZ BY YEAR-END AS CHINA CLOSES FOR LABOR DAY
snippet: GoldSeek's April 24 Asian metals update flags silver at a technical crossroads: it must close above $75.20–$75.30 post-London open to confirm a bullish trend toward $78.44 and $81.78. Bank of America maintains a silver price target of $135–$309/oz by end of 2026, while China's May 1–5 Labor Day shutdown and CME silver futures entering delivery add near-term volatility risk.

headline: COPPER NEAR $6/LB DESPITE CHINA'S RECORD SMELTER OUTPUT; TRAXYS TARGETS $15,000/TONNE FOR 2026
snippet: Copper eased 1% to $6.02/lb on April 24 as Chinese smelters posted a record 1.33 million tonnes of refined output in March, yet Goldman Sachs held its 2026 year-end price forecast unchanged while Traxys targets $15,000/tonne. The ongoing Strait of Hormuz closure is squeezing sulphuric acid supply critical to copper smelting, providing a medium-term price floor.

headline: USA RARE EARTH PAYS $2.8B FOR BRAZIL'S SERRA VERDE — WEST'S ONLY FULL-SPECTRUM MAGNETIC RARE EARTH PRODUCER
snippet: USA Rare Earth agreed to acquire Brazil's Serra Verde for $2.8B in cash and stock, securing the only mine outside Asia capable of producing all four magnetic rare earth elements at scale. Serra Verde's Pela Ema phase-one operation targets 6,400 tonnes of rare earth oxides per year; USA Rare Earth stock is up 68% year-to-date following the announcement.

headline: SOLE U.S. URANIUM CONVERTER STUDIES SECOND PLANT AS METROPOLIS BOOKS FULL THROUGH 2028
snippet: ConverDyn, operator of America's only uranium conversion facility in Metropolis, Illinois, has commissioned a leading engineering firm to evaluate a potential sister plant — Metropolis 2.0 — as the existing facility is fully booked through 2028. Output is set to hit 10,000 tonnes of UF6 in 2026, a 20% increase from 2024 levels, reflecting surging nuclear fuel demand driven by AI-related electricity growth.
